source_paper_title: On the Smoothed Complexity of Combinatorial Local Search
context:
Section 6 (Conclusion and problems), page 33

In this paper, we show that the problem of computing (pure, exact) equilibria in congestion games admits a smoothed polynomial running time algorithm for special classes of general and network congestion games (see Theorem 4.2 and Theorem 4.8, respectively). However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved.
title: Smoothed polynomial-time computation of exact pure Nash equilibria in general congestion games
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 1\) and a finite resource set \(R\) with \(|R|=m\), a (general) congestion game consists of:
- players \(N=[n]\);
- for each player \(i\in N\), a nonempty strategy set \(\Sigma_i\subseteq 2^R\);
- for each resource \(r\in R\) and load \(\ell\in[n]\), a latency \(\kappa_r(\ell)\in[0,1]\).

For a strategy profile \(\sigma=(\sigma_1,\dots,\sigma_n)\in \Sigma:=\Sigma_1\times\cdots\times\Sigma_n\), define the load on resource \(r\) by
\[\ell_r(\sigma):=|\{i\in N: r\in \sigma_i\}|\, ,\]
and the cost to player \(i\) by
\[C_i(\sigma):=\sum_{r\in \sigma_i}\kappa_r(\ell_r(\sigma)).\]
A profile \(\sigma^*\in\Sigma\) is a pure Nash equilibrium (PNE) if for all \(i\in N\) and all \(\sigma_i'\in\Sigma_i\),
\[C_i(\sigma^*)\le C_i(\sigma_i',\sigma^*_{-i}).\]

Smoothed model: for each pair \((r,\ell)\in R\times[n]\), the latency value \(\kappa_r(\ell)\) is drawn independently from a continuous distribution on \([0,1]\) with density at most \(\phi\ge 1/2\) (the distributions may be adversarially chosen subject to this density bound).

Problem: Determine whether there exists an algorithm that, for every such smoothed instance (with strategies given explicitly), computes an exact PNE in expected time polynomial in \(n\), \(m\), the encoding size of \((\Sigma_i)_{i\in N}\), and \(\phi\). Equivalently, decide whether standard better-response dynamics (for an arbitrary pivoting rule and arbitrary initial profile) converges to a PNE in an expected number of iterations bounded by a polynomial in these parameters for unrestricted congestion games (i.e., without imposing the paper's restraint/compactness conditions).
open_problem_quote: In this paper, we show that the problem of computing (pure, exact) equilibria in congestion games admits a smoothed polynomial running time algorithm for special classes of general and network congestion games (see Theorem 4.2 and Theorem 4.8, respectively). However, outside of these restricted instances of the problem, the complexity remains unresolved.
